patterns.name: Vertical SaaS Strategy
patterns.description: Focus on a specific industry vertical with deep integration and domain expertise rather than horizontal market
characteristics: Higher willingness to pay from vertical customers
patterns.recommendation: Ideal for startups with deep industry expertise. Reduces competition vs horizontal plays.

patterns.name: Low-Code/No-Code Positioning
patterns.description: Empowering non-technical users to build solutions without coding, reducing IT dependency
risks: Complexity ceiling - power users need code eventually
Maintaining ease-of-use as feature set grows
patterns.recommendation: Strong SMB/mid-market appeal. Growing trend. Easy to adopt but hard to expand upmarket.

patterns.name: Freemium to Premium Upsell
patterns.description: Acquire users with free tier, convert to paid through value realization and feature gates
characteristics: Core functionality free, advanced features paid
Low friction for free trial users
patterns.recommendation: Requires strong viral/organic growth. Works well with low-cost hosting. CAC recovery critical.

patterns.name: API-First / Platform Strategy
patterns.description: Build infrastructure/platform for developers to build on top of, creating ecosystem
patterns.recommendation: High TAM play but requires strong technical foundation and ecosystem strategy.

patterns.name: AI-Powered Efficiency / Automation
patterns.description: Use AI/ML to automate repetitive tasks or augment human capability, reducing cost or time
risks: Dependency on LLM APIs and their costs
patterns.recommendation: Hot area but crowded. Need strong defensible differentiation beyond wrapper.

patterns.name: Marketplace/Network Effects
patterns.description: Create platform connecting supply and demand, value increases with participants
patterns.recommendation: Hardest to bootstrap but strongest competitive moat. Requires capital and patient growth.
